VZ.rationale: Stable revenue and EPS growth, low-end valuation within its 1y range, and consistently positive news sentiment around its dividend and 5G initiatives make VZ a mild long lean, though thin margins and modest ROE cap the upside.
key_factors: PE diluted 17.8x, near the low end of its 1y range of 13.9–19.1x, suggesting reasonable valuation
Q1 2025 revenue 33.48B vs 32.98B YoY and EPS 1.15 vs 1.09 YoY, showing modest growth
Net margin 7.6% and ROE 10.5% are thin, limiting earnings power
News sentiment consistently positive (+1.00 on most days) with dividend and 5G contract headlines driving interest
Price down 4.6% over 3 months but up 6.0% over 1 year, showing range-bound stability

CVX.rationale: A 14% three-month pullback has brought CVX toward the middle of its valuation range, but declining quarterly revenue and EPS trajectory limit the upside and keep the call only a mild long.
key_factors: 3-month price decline of -14.4% to 143.19 creates a pullback entry, but PE at 15.964x sits mid-range (1y range 13.0-17.6), not at a discount extreme
Q1 2025 revenue of 47.61B is the weakest recent quarter vs Q4 2024's 52.23B and Q3 2024's 50.67B, with EPS of 2.00 annualizing to ~8 vs 2024's 9.72
News sentiment uniformly positive (+1.00 on 12 of 14 days) but driven by generic dividend-stock articles rather than company-specific catalysts
Chevron's lithium deal (125,000-acre Texas-Arkansas, 2025-06-23) is a long-term diversification positive but immaterial to near-term earnings

sources_cited.excerpt: net sentiment +1.00 (n=3); 12 of 14 sentiment days at +1.00
Chevron Joins Lithium Race With 125,000-Acre Texas-Arkansas Deal

CRM.rationale: Valuation has compressed to the low end of its one-year range while sentiment stays positive and revenue growth persists, but the P/E of 42.9x still prices in substantial execution risk with margins under pressure.
key_factors: P/E diluted at 42.9x sits near the low end of its 37.1–77.7x one-year range, offering relative value vs own history
Revenue grew from 9.44B (Q3 FY25) to 9.83B (Q1 FY26) with stable gross margin at 77.2%
Operating margin of 19% and net margin of 16.4% remain modest for the valuation, limiting upside surprise potential
News sentiment net score has been consistently positive (mostly +1.00) through June 2025, supporting a constructive near-term backdrop

JPM.rationale: Solid fundamentals and reasonable absolute valuation support ownership, but the stock sits at the top of its 1-year PE range after a +45.6% run, capping near-term upside and making risk-adjusted reward only mildly positive.
key_factors: PE at top of 1y range: pe_diluted=15.45x vs 1y range 10.4..15.5, limiting valuation upside after +45.6% 1y return
Solid Q1 2025 YoY growth: revenue 45.31B (+8% vs 41.93B Q1'24), EPS 5.07 (+14% vs 4.44 Q1'24)
Strong profitability: net_margin=0.31, roe=0.155, operating_margin=0.398 support continued earnings power
Momentum risk: ret_3m=+18.2% with price rising from ~260 to 289.91 in six weeks increases pullback risk

PG.rationale: High-quality consumer staples trading at the bottom of its recent valuation range after a 6.5% pullback, with strong margins, positive sentiment, and no fundamental deterioration — reward skews modestly ahead of risk.
key_factors: PE at 25.0x sits at the low end of its 1y range (24.8–30.8), a meaningful de-rating after the -6.5% 3-month pullback
Strong profitability: ROE 29.7%, net margin 18.6%, gross margin 51.3%, operating margin 23.8% — high-quality compounder at a compressed multiple
News sentiment overwhelmingly positive (12 of 15 days scored +1.00, zero negative days), suggesting no deteriorating fundamental narrative
Q3 revenue 19.78B vs Q2 21.88B reflects normal P&G seasonality (Jan-Mar weaker than holiday Q2), not structural decline

MMM.rationale: Reasonable 19x PE with solid 17.9% net margins and positive end-market growth signals are offset by a declining revenue trajectory and high 3x debt-to-equity leverage, making for a mild long lean with limited conviction.
key_factors: PE diluted at 19.05x sits mid-range of its 1y band (16.7–20.5), not expensive for 17.9% net margins and 18.6% operating margins
Revenue declining sequentially: Q3'24 6.29B → Q4'24 6.01B → Q1'25 5.95B, signaling top-line pressure
High leverage with debt-to-equity of 2.979 and total debt of 13.48B against only 4.52B equity limits financial flexibility
Positive news sentiment (consistently +1.00) with growth in key end markets — indoor air quality (8.09% CAGR to 2032), data center filters, advanced ceramics ($16.27B by 2029)

revenue=5.95B net_income=1.12B eps_diluted=2.04 gross_profit=2.48B operating_income=1.25B total_debt=13.48B total_equity=4.52B
Indoor Air Quality Monitor Market Size to Hit USD 9.38 Billion by 2032, at a CAGR of 8.09%
Advanced Ceramics Market worth $16.27 billion by 2029, at 6.0%

IBM.rationale: IBM trades at 50.9x diluted PE — the top of its one-year range — on only 8.7% net margins and modest revenue growth, making the risk of multiple compression outweigh the momentum-driven reward over a one-year horizon.
key_factors: PE at 50.92x sits at the very top of its 1y range (19.5–50.9), pricing in growth the fundamentals don't yet deliver
Stock already up +72.5% over the past year and +18.5% in 3 months — much of the AI/quantum narrative is priced in
Q1 2025 revenue of $14.54B with EPS of $1.12 shows no explosive growth; operating margin only 9.4%
News sentiment overwhelmingly positive (+1.00 on most days in June) but driven by thematic hype (quantum, AI, blockchain) rather than concrete fundamental acceleration

JNJ.rationale: At ~17x PE with 24% net margins and 28% ROE, JNJ offers reasonable risk-adjusted value after a ~8% three-month pullback, supported by positive pipeline readouts, though tepid revenue growth and patent-cliff overhang cap the upside.
key_factors: PE diluted at 16.975x sits in the lower-middle of its 1y range (9.2–29.0), attractive for a defensive pharma with 24.4% net margin and 27.9% ROE
3-month pullback of -7.9% from ~157 to 152.75 improves entry point
Q1 2025 revenue 21.89B shows slight sequential decline from Q4 2024's 22.52B, signaling tepid top-line growth
Positive pipeline news flow: nipocalimab ITC data for gMG, talquetamab+teclistamab combo in myeloma, ASCO blood cancer data — sentiment net positive through late June
June 27 bearish article ('Great. Why You Shouldn't Buy It.') introduces caution but is a single data point against otherwise positive sentiment

sources_cited.excerpt: Nipocalimab showed greater sustained disease control versus approved FcRn blockers for gMG at multiple timepoints over 24 weeks
TALVEY and TECVAYLI combination shows deep and durable responses in heavily pretreated multiple myeloma patients
Johnson & Johnson Is Great. Here's Why You Shouldn't Buy It.

NKE.rationale: Nike's Q4 earnings beat and 'Sport Offense' turnaround narrative are encouraging, but the stock has already rallied ~15% on the news and fundamentals remain depressed with declining quarterly EPS and compressed margins, making the risk/reward only mildly positive.
key_factors: Q4 earnings beat on June 26 drove stock from ~62.54 to ~72.04 (+15%), but much of the good news is now priced in at PE 23.3x
Quarterly EPS trajectory deteriorating: 0.78 (Nov 2024) → 0.54 (Feb 2025), and operating margin compressed to 11.1% vs Nike's historical 13-15%
Gross margin at 43.8% remains below Nike's historical norm, signaling continued pricing/wholesale pressure
Management signaled headwinds expected to moderate and is playing 'Sport Offense' — turnaround is early-stage but directionally positive
Stock down 24.6% over past year, providing a low base and potential mean-reversion upside if turnaround executes

sources_cited.excerpt: Nike Q4 Earnings: Revenue Beat, EPS Beat, Headwinds Expected To Moderate Moving Forward, Company Playing 'Sport Offense'
Is It Time to Just Buy Nike Stock as a Turnaround Takes Hold?

KO.rationale: KO offers a defensive profile with strong margins and high ROE, but its 28.3x PE against sequentially declining revenue limits the risk-adjusted reward to a mild long lean after the recent pullback.
key_factors: PE at 28.3x sits mid-range of its 1y band (25.1–30.0), limiting both upside and downside — not cheap, not extreme
Q1 2025 revenue $11.13B declined sequentially from Q3 2024's $11.85B and Q4 2024's $11.54B, but EPS rebounded to $0.77 from $0.51 in Q4
Strong profitability supports the premium: net margin 23%, ROE 38.9%, gross margin 61.1%, operating margin 24.5%
Stock pulled back ~1.2% over 3 months from ~72 to 70.75 with uniformly positive news sentiment (+1.00 on most days), driven by dividend/Buffett narratives

V.rationale: Visa's elite 53% net margins and 52% ROE underpin a durable franchise, but flat quarterly revenue and a 33% one-year run-up leave limited upside relative to risk after the recent pullback.
key_factors: Exceptional profitability: net_margin=0.529, operating_margin=0.637, roe=0.523 — among the best in any industry
Revenue growth is modest: quarterly rev flat at 9.59B vs 9.51B prior quarter, limiting earnings acceleration potential
Stock up +33.2% over past year but recently pulled back ~5% from 373.31 (June 11) to 355.05 (June 30), offering a slightly better entry
News sentiment predominantly positive (net scores mostly +1.00) with bullish Buffett/long-term-hold framing, though no catalyst-driven upside visible

NVDA.rationale: Exceptional fundamental momentum (47% YoY revenue growth, 70% gross margins) combined with a PE that has compressed from 70x to 50x makes the risk-reward mildly attractive, though the rich multiple and recent 46% run-up cap conviction.
key_factors: Q1 FY26 revenue $44.06B vs $30.04B prior-year quarter implies ~47% YoY growth, with 70.1% gross margin and 58% operating margin — exceptional profitability at scale
PE diluted 50.6x sits below its 1y peak of 70.0x, meaning earnings growth is outpacing price appreciation despite +45.8% 3-month return
Net margin 51.7% and ROE 91.6% with debt-to-equity of just 0.101 show a fortress balance sheet supporting the premium multiple
News sentiment consistently positive (+0.67 to +1.00 over last two weeks of June) with no negative catalysts visible in the data

MSFT.rationale: Exceptional business quality (36% net margin, net cash, sequential EPS growth) supports a premium, but at 39.3x PE near the top of its 1y range after a +32.5% three-month run, much of the good news is already priced in, leaving a balanced-to-mildly-positive risk/reward.
key_factors: Valuation near top of 1y range: PE 39.3x vs 29.3–40.5 range, EV/EBIT 31.3x vs 23.1–32.7 range — limited valuation cushion after +32.5% 3-month rally
Strong fundamentals: net margin 36%, operating margin 44.8%, EPS grew to 3.46 from 3.30 sequentially, net cash position (cash 79.62B vs debt 42.88B)
Positive news sentiment sustained: daily scores +0.5 to +1.0 through late June with no negative spikes
1-year return only +9.8% despite recent surge, suggesting the stock was lagging earlier in the year and the recent rally is catching up to fundamentals

CSCO.rationale: Stock trades at the peak of its 1-year valuation range (28.4x PE) after a 46% rally, but fundamentals show only modest revenue growth and declining EPS — the multiple expansion is not supported by accelerating fundamentals.
key_factors: PE at top of 1y range: pe_diluted=28.356 vs 1y range 15.0..28.4 after +46.2% 1y price run-up
Modest revenue growth with declining EPS: rev 13.84B→13.99B→14.15B but EPS 0.68→0.61→0.62 over last 3 quarters
Solid but static margins: gross_margin=0.652, operating_margin=0.203, net_margin=0.176 — no expansion to justify multiple re-rating
Positive but generic news flow: sentiment consistently +1.00 but headlines are mostly market-research reports, not company-specific catalysts

AMZN.rationale: Amazon's valuation has compressed to the lower half of its recent PE range while profitability and balance-sheet quality continue to improve, making it a mild long — though the recent 15% rally tempers the risk-adjusted reward.
key_factors: PE at 35.9x sits in lower half of its 1y range (30.3–56.6x), offering relative value for a mega-cap with expanding margins
Q1 2025 net margin ~11% (net_income=17.13B on rev=155.67B) and operating margin 11% show profitability acceleration vs prior quarters
Low leverage (debt_to_equity=0.192) and strong ROE (0.216) underpin balance-sheet quality
News sentiment consistently positive (latest +0.83 on 2025-06-30) but stock already up +15.3% in 3 months, limiting near-term upside

AMGN.rationale: Valuation has compressed to the low end of its range with improving revenue growth, but an 8x debt-to-equity ratio and volatile EPS make the risk-reward only mildly attractive.
key_factors: PE at 35.8x near low end of 1y range (33.1–58.3), some valuation compression already priced in after -11% 1y return
Q1 2025 revenue 8.15B up ~9.4% YoY vs 7.45B in Q1 2024, with EPS recovering to 3.20 from -0.21
Extremely leveraged balance sheet: total_debt=57.38B vs total_equity=6.21B, debt_to_equity=8.0x — dominant risk
MariTide shows promise but dose issues create pipeline execution uncertainty per June 24 analyst note

sources_cited.excerpt: Analyst Views Mixed As Amgen's Weight Loss/Diabetes Drug MariTide Shows Promise But Has Dose Issues

HD.rationale: HD offers a reasonable risk/reward at 24.9x earnings with solid margins and a strategic acquisition, but modest revenue growth and consumer macro sensitivity cap the upside, warranting only a mild long lean.
key_factors: PE at 24.9x sits mid-range of its 1y band (22.3–29.3), not cheap but not stretched for a high-ROE (184%) franchise
Q1 2025 revenue $39.86B only modestly grew vs prior-year comparable quarters ($39.70B Q4, $40.22B Q3), signaling low single-digit growth
$5.5B GMS acquisition expands distribution arm — potential growth catalyst but adds integration and balance-sheet risk
Stock dropped ~9% from mid-May to mid-June (380→347) before recovering to 366, suggesting selling pressure may be largely absorbed

sources_cited.excerpt: Home Depot's Distribution Arm Expands With $5.5 billion GMS Deal

MRK.rationale: High-quality pharma with 27.3% net margin and 36.1% ROE trading at just 11.5x PE after a 39% one-year drawdown — the valuation compression appears to overdiscount the Keytruda patent-cliff risk while new FDA approvals extend the franchise.
key_factors: PE diluted at 11.48x, near the low end of its 1-year range (10.7–142.4), despite 27.3% net margin and 36.1% ROE — cheap for this quality
Stock down 39.0% over the past year (ret_1y=-39.0%), pricing in significant pessimism
Keytruda secured FDA nod for head and neck cancer as first immunotherapy around surgery (2025-06-13), extending the franchise
Sequential revenue decline: Q3'24 16.66B → Q4'24 15.62B → Q1'25 15.53B, the key risk to the thesis

Merck's Keytruda Secures FDA Nod For Head And Neck Cancer As First Immunotherapy For Use Around Surgery
RFK Jr's CDC Panel Recommends Merck's RSV Shot For Infants

UNH.rationale: UNH has been crushed 40% in three months yet trades at just 12.5x earnings with 22.8% ROE — the valuation compression is extreme for a dominant healthcare franchise, making the risk-reward favorable even with lawsuit and margin overhangs.
key_factors: PE diluted at 12.5x, near the low end of its 1y range of 11.0–38.7x, after a -40.4% 3-month drawdown — extreme valuation compression for a dominant franchise
ROE of 22.8% and gross margin of 88.3% show the underlying business remains highly profitable despite operating margin compression to 8.2%
Q1 2025 EPS of 6.85 up from Q4 2024's 5.98, with revenue of 109.58B showing continued top-line growth
Class action lawsuits and prior-authorization profit concerns create near-term overhang, but P/S of 0.698 and P/B of 2.841 suggest much of the bad news is priced in

sources_cited.excerpt: Class Action Lawsuits Have Been Filed Against UnitedHealth
Will It Cut Profits Even More?
3 Reasons Warren Buffett Might Buy UnitedHealth Group Stock

GS.rationale: Strong sequential fundamental momentum (Q1'25 EPS 14.12 vs 8.40 in Q3'24) at a reasonable 15.4x PE is attractive, but the +58.7% 1-year price surge tempers the reward-to-risk into a mild long lean.
key_factors: Sequential quarterly EPS improvement: 8.40 → 11.90 → 14.12 from Q3'24 to Q1'25, with revenue rising 12.70B → 13.87B → 15.06B
PE at 15.4x sits mid-range of its 1y band (11.4–18.4), not stretched after the run-up
Strong 3-month price momentum (+29.6%) and +58.7% 1y return create pullback risk near 707.75
Operating margin 34.6% and net margin 27.4% reflect healthy profitability

BA.rationale: Boeing's negative equity, 53.4B debt load, recurring safety crises (Air India 787 crash), and legal investigations outweigh Q1's modest operational improvement, making the risk/reward unattractive at 2.27x sales with deeply negative margins.
key_factors: Negative book equity of -3.33B with 53.39B total debt — balance sheet is deeply stressed
Air India 787 crash on June 12 rekindled safety doubts; stock plunged from 217.51 (Jun 9) to 197.68 (Jun 18)
Multiple shareholder investigation announcements (Pomerantz Jun 18 & Jun 28, Bragar Eagel Jun 19) add legal overhang
Q1 2025 showed improvement (EPS -0.16 vs Q4 2024 -5.45, operating income 461M) but full-year 2024 EPS was -18.36
Stock trades at 2.27x sales despite negative net margin (-16.6%) and negative gross margin (-2.1%) — valuation rich for a loss-making company

sources_cited.excerpt: Boeing Stock Plunges After Air India 787 Crash Rekindles Safety Doubts
INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of The Boeing Company - BA

MCD.rationale: Declining quarterly revenue and EPS trajectory combined with still-elevated ~28x valuation makes risk-reward unattractive despite strong margins and brand quality.
key_factors: Declining fundamental trajectory: EPS fell from 3.13 (Q3'24) to 2.80 (Q4'24) to 2.60 (Q1'25), revenue from 6.87B to 6.39B to 5.96B over same quarters
Valuation still elevated at ~28x annualized EPS (2.60×4=10.40 vs price 292.17), near top of 1y range pe_diluted 0.0..28.2
Strong margins persist (operating_margin=0.452, net_margin=0.318) but don't offset deteriorating top-line and bottom-line trend
Stock down 6.5% in 3 months but 1y return still +13.2%, suggesting partial but incomplete de-rating

sources_cited.excerpt: net sentiment -1.00 on 2025-06-11 (Snapchat child safety concerns)

HON.rationale: HON trades at the top of its 1-year PE range (25.2x) with declining revenue and flat EPS, meaning positive macro sentiment and defense/automation tailwinds appear already priced in, leaving limited upside against balance-sheet and valuation risk.
key_factors: PE at 25.2x, top of 1y range (19.8–25.5), with P/B of 8.0x — valuation stretched vs. own history
Q1 2025 revenue $8.93B down ~2% YoY from $9.11B; EPS flat at $2.22 vs $2.23 — no earnings growth to justify multiple expansion
Strong profitability (operating margin 20.1%, ROE 31.7%) provides a floor but is not improving
Debt/equity 1.54 with $27B total debt adds balance-sheet risk at premium valuation
News sentiment consistently +1.00 with BofA bullish on military spending and durable goods +16.5%, but tailwinds appear already discounted

sources_cited.excerpt: Honeywell, ITT, Eaton See Bright Outlook As BofA Securities Cites Military Spending, Aftermarket Growth

TRV.rationale: Sharp Q1 2025 earnings deterioration (EPS 1.7 vs 8.96 prior quarter) with no valuation cushion at 14.4x mid-range PE and fading price momentum makes the risk/reward slightly unattractive over the next year.
key_factors: Q1 2025 EPS collapsed to 1.7 from 8.96 in Q4 2024 and 5.42 in Q3 2024, with net margin compressing to ~3.3% vs trailing 9.1%
PE at 14.4x sits mid-range of its 1y band (10.9–16.3), offering no valuation cushion against earnings deterioration
Stock up +29.3% over the past year but has rolled over from ~276 to 267 since early June, suggesting momentum is fading into the weak print
Recent news sentiment turned positive (+1.00 on 2025-04-16 and 2025-05-28), a mild offset but not enough to counter the fundamental decline

DIS.rationale: Improving EPS trajectory and a PE near the low end of its own 1-year range make DIS mildly attractive, though a +25.6% three-month rally into 52-week highs limits upside conviction.
key_factors: EPS improving sharply across recent quarters: 0.26 → 1.40 → 1.81, signaling operational recovery
PE diluted at 23.8x sits near the low end of its 1-year range (19.6–64.4), suggesting valuation is not stretched relative to recent history
Operating margin of 18.5% and net margin of 10% indicate solid profitability supporting the earnings rebound
Stock has rallied +25.6% in 3 months to a 52-week high at 124.01, partially pricing in the improvement and limiting near-term reward
News sentiment turned positive in late June (+1.00 on both 6/28 and 6/30), with headlines highlighting 52-week highs and a strong post-pandemic year for entertainment stocks

sources_cited.excerpt: net sentiment +1.00 on 6/28 and 6/30; headlines: 'Can Disney Stock Keep Rising After Hitting a New 52-Week High?' and 'Could 2025 Be the Best Post-Pandemic Year for Entertainment Stocks?'

WMT.rationale: WMT's defensive quality and consistent growth support a modest long, but a PE of 40.4x at the top of its 1-year range (27.5–41.5) after a +44% one-year run leaves limited multiple-expansion upside and caps risk-adjusted reward.
key_factors: PE diluted at 40.4x, near the top of its 1-year range of 27.5–41.5, limiting valuation upside
Strong 1-year price return of +44.0% suggests much of the growth story is already priced in
Solid fundamentals: ROE 21.6%, revenue $165.6B in Q1 with seasonal dip from $180.6B holiday quarter, consistent EPS trajectory ($0.57→$0.66→$0.56)
News flow is neutral with no WMT-specific catalysts; sentiment scores mostly +0.00 to +1.00 but low article count

CAT.rationale: CAT trades at the top of its 1-year valuation range (18.6x PE) after a sharp 3-month rally, while Q1 2025 fundamentals show clear deceleration in both revenue and EPS versus prior quarters, making risk outweigh reward near-term.
key_factors: PE at top of 1y range: pe_diluted=18.639 vs 12.4 low — valuation stretched after +17.7% 3-month rally
Q1 2025 deceleration: revenue=14.25B and eps=4.2 vs Q4 2024 rev=16.21B/eps=5.78 and Q3 2024 rev=16.11B/eps=5.06
Strong margins and ROE (net_margin=0.157, roe=0.55) provide a floor but are already reflected in the elevated multiple
Positive news flow (dividend increase, dollar weakness tailwind, mining automation demand) largely priced into the recent run-up

AAPL.rationale: At 31.75x earnings with essentially flat revenue growth, deteriorating sentiment, and a securities fraud lawsuit overhang, the risk-reward is modestly unattractive despite Apple's fortress margins and ongoing de-rating.
key_factors: PE at 31.75x remains expensive for ~0.5% YoY revenue growth (Q2 FY25 rev=95.36B vs prior comparable quarter 94.93B)
Stock de-rating underway: ret_3m=-7.6%, ret_1y=-4.2%, PE fallen from 42.6x high to 31.75x but still above 27.2x low of range
News sentiment deteriorating from +1.00 (mid-June) to +0.17 (Jun 30), with securities fraud class action filed Jun 29
Strong margins (gross 46.6%, operating 31.8%, net 24.3%) and ROE 145.7% provide a fundamental floor but don't justify the multiple at current growth

sources_cited.excerpt: 2025-06-29 AAPL Investor Alert: A Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against Apple Inc.

SHW.rationale: Recent ~5% pullback from 360 to 343 with persistently positive news sentiment offers a mild entry improvement, but a 32x PE against flat sequential revenue growth and 2.17x debt-to-equity limits the risk-adjusted reward.
key_factors: PE diluted at 32.3x, mid-range of 29.7–39.7 1y band — premium for modest growth (Q1'25 rev 5.31B vs Q4'24 5.30B, essentially flat)
Stock dropped from ~360 to ~336 on June 12-13 and only partially recovered to 343.36 — pullback creates mild value improvement
ROE 64.9% is strong but amplified by leverage: D/E=2.17, total debt 8.98B vs equity 4.13B
News sentiment consistently +1.00 through May 2025 with zero negative reads — no fundamental deterioration evident behind June selloff

AXP.rationale: Excellent franchise quality (32.9% ROE, growing EPS) supports a mild long, but valuation near the top of its 1-year range after a +39.7% run-up caps the risk-adjusted reward.
key_factors: PE diluted 21.79x near top of 1-year range (16.5–23.4) after +39.7% 1-year price return
ROE 32.9%, operating margin 33.4%, net margin 26.3% — top-tier profitability
Q1 2025 EPS $3.64 vs Q4 2024 $3.04 (+20% sequential), annualized ~$14.56 vs 2024 full-year $14.01
Sharp recent surge: +18.6% in 3 months including +11% in last 2 weeks of June (287.79→318.98)
